title: EMPLOYMENT RELATED DAYCARE - 211info
text: SOUTHEAST PORTLAND SELF-SUFFICIENCY OFFICE EMPLOYMENT RELATED DAYCARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Employment Related Day Care program (ERDC) helps families who are working, in school, or receiving Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) pay for child care, including registration fees. 8129 SE Malden Street Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: This program is for families that: -Live in Oregon -Are working or in school or receiving TANF -In a two-parent family, both parents must be working or in school. (This does not apply to parents receiving TANF.) -There are some exceptions for two parent households where one parent cannot provide child care because of a medical or mental health condition, or if supervised contact is required by the State -Need child care to keep working, attending school, or participating in family well-being activities Have a child who is: -Age 13 or younger and needs child care, or -Ages 13-17 and needs care because of special circumstances -Meet income limits. -Initial income limits 200% federal poverty level (FPL). Ongoing and exit limit 250% FPL. Hours: Office is temporarily closed (971) 673-2550 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (971) 673-2550 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Apply online, by phone, or in person at a local ODHS Self-Sufficiency Branch Office. Visit website for more information and to view income chart. FEES: ERDC is a subsidy program. This means many families still pay part of the child care cost. This is called a copayment (copay). The copay amount depends on family size and income. DOCUMENTS: The documents required for verification will vary by family situation LANGUAGES: Phone: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement Application: Available in English, English Large Print, Spanish, Spanish Large Print, Arabic, Burmese, Nepali, Russian, Somali, Vietnamese AREA SERVED: Multnomah County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located off of 82nd and Flavel behind the 7-Eleven store Services: Child Care Expense Assistance Child Care Expense Assistance * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Other Services Offered At This Location: PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SNAP PROGRAM PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SUMMER EBT PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE FOR NEEDY FAMILIES TEMPORARY FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE TO DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SURVIVORS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. SOUTHEAST PORTLAND SELF-SUFFICIENCY OFFICE
